vectors.name: Unary Minus Operator (-)
vectors.description: Negates the value of a numeric operand.

quirks_and_tests.description: Evaluates at Level 1 (highest precedence) along with NOT and @. It is a unary sign operator, distinct from binary subtraction.

vectors.name: Address Operator (@)
vectors.description: Returns the memory address of a variable, procedure, or function.

quirks_and_tests.description: Evaluates at Level 1 precedence. Generates a pointer to the memory location of the operand.

vectors.name: Dereference Operator (^)
vectors.description: Accesses the value stored at the memory address pointed to by a typed pointer.

quirks_and_tests.description: A postfix operator. Modifying the dereferenced pointer directly mutates the original variable residing at that memory address.

vectors.name: Multiplication Operator (*)
vectors.syntax: Operand1: Numeric * Operand2: Numeric; { Returns Numeric }

quirks_and_tests.description: Level 2 precedence. If both operands are integers, returns an integer. If one is real, returns a real. (Note: Also used for Set intersection, not covered here).

vectors.name: Real Division Operator (/)
vectors.syntax: Operand1: Numeric / Operand2: Numeric; { Returns Real }

quirks_and_tests.description: Level 2 precedence. The division operator (/) ALWAYS returns a Real type, even if both operands are integers and the division is mathematically exact.

vectors.name: Addition Operator (+)
vectors.syntax: Operand1: Numeric + Operand2: Numeric; { Returns Numeric }

quirks_and_tests.description: Level 3 precedence. (Note: Also used for Set union, not covered here).

vectors.name: Subtraction Operator (-)
vectors.syntax: Operand1: Numeric - Operand2: Numeric; { Returns Numeric }

quirks_and_tests.description: Level 3 precedence. Evaluates left-to-right when chained with addition. (Note: Also used for Set difference, not covered here).

vectors.name: String Concatenation Operator (+)
vectors.syntax: Operand1: String + Operand2: String; { Returns String }
vectors.description: The addition operator is overloaded to perform string concatenation when applied to String or Char types.

quirks_and_tests.description: Concatenates dynamically. If the resulting string exceeds 255 characters, it is silently truncated at runtime.

vectors.name: Equality Operator (=)
vectors.description: Evaluates to True if both expressions are equal.

quirks_and_tests.description: Level 4 precedence (lowest). String comparisons are strictly case-sensitive.

vectors.name: Inequality Operator (<>)
vectors.description: Evaluates to True if expressions are not equal.

vectors.name: Less Than Operator (<)
vectors.description: Evaluates to True if the left expression is strictly less than the right.

vectors.name: Greater Than Operator (>)
vectors.description: Evaluates to True if the left expression is strictly greater than the right.

vectors.name: Less Than or Equal Operator (<=)
vectors.description: Evaluates to True if the left expression is less than or equal to the right.

quirks_and_tests.description: Level 4 precedence. (Note: Also used to test if Set1 is a subset of Set2).

vectors.name: Greater Than or Equal Operator (>=)
vectors.description: Evaluates to True if the left expression is greater than or equal to the right.

quirks_and_tests.description: Level 4 precedence. (Note: Also used to test if Set1 is a superset of Set2).

vectors.name: Set Membership Operator (IN)
vectors.description: Evaluates to True if the element is a member of the specified set.

quirks_and_tests.description: Level 4 precedence. Operates on ordinal types and sets.

vectors.name: Operator Precedence Matrix
vectors.description: Defines the strict hierarchy of expression evaluation. Operators with higher precedence are evaluated first.

quirks_and_tests.description: CRITICAL COMPILE-TIME QUIRK: Logical operators (AND/OR, Level 2/3) have higher precedence than relational operators (=/<, Level 4). Expressions like 'A = 1 AND B = 2' will throw a Type Mismatch. They MUST be parenthesized: '(A = 1) AND (B = 2)'. This test validates the full chain.
setup: PrecChainFull := PrecChain1 AND PrecChain2 AND PrecChain3 AND (3 IN [1..5]);
